threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think paying for the bus is a novelty instead of a soul-crushing necessity. Locals know, it’s just part of the grind.
Last week on the 35, a lad started singing the whole of 'Wonderwall' and everyone joined in. Never thought I'd see Brixton turn a bus into a karaoke night.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a kid selling handmade bracelets at the U-Bahn entrance while his grandma bartered for vegetables at the stand next door.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Friedrichshain, you'll find spontaneous dance parties in the street when the sun comes out. No permit, just locals vibing together. It's a vibe!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they’ve got a favorite taco truck but still complain about the 14 Mission being a total nightmare at rush hour.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy on the 21 bus doing a full karaoke set while the driver rolled his eyes. Only in the Tenderloin would that feel like normal.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the mosaics at Park Güell are the pinnacle of art in Gràcia, but they miss the real gems—local murals tucked away in side streets.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newcomer by their bewilderment over the tram schedules—those shiny maps don’t help when the trams have a mind of their own.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the number 30 bus will actually get you anywhere on time. Welcome to no-man’s land, mate.
Caught the 68 home last night, and a couple was arguing about whether they should move to Brixton. The driver just kept shaking his head.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I spotted the busker playing Bach on a violin in the U-Bahn, while two kids danced around him. That’s when it hit me—this place has soul.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you spot a techno party in a former factory while the U-Bahn shakes the walls. It's a vibe that catches you off guard.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they know to avoid the bus stop at 16th and Mission after 5 PM—absolute chaos, a perfect storm of delays and street performers.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a street musician set up right next to the Muni delay, totally captured the mood. His sax hit a perfect note just as the bus finally rolled in.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about parks and plazas, but it’s the hidden tapas bars in the alleys that show the real local vibe.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by how they still stop for every red light, even when there’s no traffic. True locals know the signal's just a suggestion.
